etymology.description: 亻(亻) (rén, "person") + 矦(侯) (hóu, ancient form, "target for arrows") = combined human element with phonetic cue from 侯(侯) to imply waiting or time-related activities
etymology.interpretation: The character 候 was designed to convey a sense of humans involved with waiting or being on the lookout, borrowing the sound and visual elements from 侯(侯), which represented a target for arrows, suggesting observation or anticipation.

examples.chinese: 请稍等一会儿(請稍等一會兒)。
我们在车站等候(我們在車站等候)，汽车却迟迟未来(汽車卻遲遲未來)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài chēzhàn děnghòu, qìchē què chíchí wèi lái.
examples.english: We waited at the station, but the bus did not come for a long time.

examples.chinese: 工作繁忙的候(工作繁忙的候)，他总是很疲倦(他總是很疲倦)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngzuò fánmáng de hòu, tā zǒng shì hěn píjuàn.
examples.english: During busy work periods, he always feels tired.
